content:
🌟 browserytools | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Providing a private, offline suite of essential browser-based tools for everyday productivity tasks like file conversion, image editing, text manipulation, and data processing.
✨ Key Features: • AI-powered background removal • Image compression and format conversion • PDF manipulation (merge, split, compress)
📖 Summary: BrowseryTools is a collection of over 80 browser-based utility tools built with Next.js and TypeScript. These tools operate entirely client-side, ensuring user privacy and eliminating the need for server uploads or accounts. They cover a wide range of functionalities including image manipulation, file conversion, text processing, data handling, and calculations.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 linux-wallpaperengine | C++
🎯 Primary Use Case: To enable users to use animated wallpapers from Steam's Wallpaper Engine on Linux desktops.
✨ Key Features: • Run Steam Wallpaper Engine backgrounds on Linux • OpenGL-based implementation • Supports X11 and Wayland
📖 Summary: This project brings Wallpaper Engine-style live wallpapers to Linux, allowing users to run animated wallpapers from Steam's Wallpaper Engine on their desktop. It's an educational OpenGL-based wallpaper engine for Linux.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 unity-mcp | C#
🎯 Primary Use Case: Enabling AI assistants to directly interact with and control the Unity Editor for game development and other Unity-based projects.
✨ Key Features: • AI assistant integration (Claude, Cursor, VS Code, etc.) • Direct Unity Editor interaction via MCP • Tools for asset management • Tools for scene control • Tools for script editing
📖 Summary: Unity MCP is a C# integration tool that bridges AI assistants with the Unity Editor. It allows LLMs to directly manage Unity assets, control scenes, edit scripts, and automate development tasks through the Model Context Protocol.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ Constrict | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Compressing video files to meet specific size limits for uploads or storage, without manual trial-and-error.
✨ Key Features: • Compress videos to target file sizes • Automatic calculation of ABR, resolution, framerate, and audio quality • Bulk compression of multiple videos • Customization of framerate limits • Choice of codecs (H.264, HEVC, AV1, VP9)
📖 Summary: Constrict is a desktop application designed to compress video files to a specific target file size. It automates the process of calculating necessary encoding parameters like bitrate, resolution, and framerate, offering a user-friendly interface for bulk video compression.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
